title: Circular Shift
description:
Circular shift the digits of the integer x, shift the digits right by shift
    and return the result as a string.
    If shift > number of digits, return digits reversed.
    >>> circular_shift(12, 1)
    "21"
    >>> circular_shift(12, 2)
    "12"
skeleton_code:
def circular_shift(x, shift):
    """Circular shift the digits of the integer x, shift the digits right by shift
    and return the result as a string.
    If shift > number of digits, return digits reversed.
    >>> circular_shift(12, 1)
    "21"
    >>> circular_shift(12, 2)
    "12"
    """"""
    pass

expected: This prints if this assert fails 1 (good for debugging!)
This prints if this assert fails 2 (also good for debugging!)

source.paper: Chen, M., Tworek, J., Jun, H., Yuan, Q., Pinto, H.P.O., Kaplan, J., Edwards, H., Burda, Y., Joseph, N., Brockman, G., et al. (2021). Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code. arXiv:2107.03374

reference_solution:
def circular_shift(x, shift):
    """Circular shift the digits of the integer x, shift the digits right by shift
    and return the result as a string.
    If shift > number of digits, return digits reversed.
    >>> circular_shift(12, 1)
    "21"
    >>> circular_shift(12, 2)
    "12"
    """
    s = str(x)
    if shift > len(s):
        return s[::-1]
    else:
        return s[len(s) - shift:] + s[:len(s) - shift]
